zh.lifeSummary: 當Jan I van der Lede於1205年出生在荷蘭-拉藍德阿可爾時，他的父親Floris Herbaren van der Lede35歲，母親Asekijn Huges Bottersdr van Schoonhoven26歲。他於1230年之前與Jutta在艾歐洛結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於1272年11月24日之前在荷蘭。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Jan I van der Lede于1205年出生在荷兰-拉藍德阿可爾时，他的父亲Floris Herbaren van der Lede35岁，母亲Asekijn Huges Bottersdr van Schoonhoven26岁。他于1230年之前与Jutta在艾歐洛结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和2个女儿的父母。他去世于1272年11月24日之前在荷兰。

nameMeanings.name: van
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Anh, Thanh, Hung, Minh, Vinh, Dung, Hue, Huong, Lan, Quang, Tan, Thuy, Hong, Chi, Hang, Ho, Pok, Sang, Bai, Chin, Ching, Chu, Chuan, Dong.
Vietnamese (Vạn): from the Chinese surname 萬, see Wan 1.
Vietnamese (Văn): from the Chinese surnames 文 or 聞, see Wen 2 and 3, and also 5 below.

variants.name: Wan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Hong, Yi, Qi, Qiang, Wai, Yiu, Yuk, Chien-Min, Fang, Hin, Jichun, Li, Man, Chung, Min, Yiping, Pak, Sik, Sung Man.  Vietnamese Tin, Bang, Da, Hien, Hoi, Lan, Minh Quang, Sau, Tich, Tung, Yaping, Yuet.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 萬, which means ‘ten thousand’ literally or ‘very great number’ figuratively: (i) from the personal name of Bi Wan (畢萬), an official in the state of Jin (located in present-day Shanxi province). He was said to be a descendant of Bi Gong Gao (known as Duke of Bi), the 15th son of the virtuous King Wen of Zhou (reined 1152–1056  BC ). Bi Wan was later granted the fiefdom of Wei (魏), which in 403  BC  became the state of Wei (located in present-day Shanxi, Shaanxi, Henan and Hebei provinces). Some of his descendants adopted Wei (魏) as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his personal name, as their surname. (ii) from the personal name Wan (萬), style name of Rui Bo (芮伯, the Earl of Rui), an official of the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ), who was granted the fief Rui (located in Ruicheng in Shaanxi province). Some of his descendants inherited Rui (芮), the name of the fief, as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his style name, as their surname. (iii) from the placename Wan (萬), the name of a fief (located in Juxian in Shandong province) granted during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ) to an official in the ancient state of Chu. (iv) from the first element of the name of the Wan Si (萬俟) family, who lived in the downstream areas of the Yellow River during the Northern Qi dynasty (550–577  AD ). (v) from the second element of the name of the Tu Wan (吐萬) family, who are members of the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 完, meaning ‘complete’ in Chinese: (i) said to be borne by the descendants of Huan Zhui, a grandson of the Duke Huan of Song (ruler of the state of Song, died 651  BC ). (ii) from Wan (完), the name of Qu Wan (屈完), an official in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn Period (770–476  BC ). (iii) shortened form of the compound surname Wan-Yan (完顔), a surname from minority ethnic groups such as Juchen ethnic group in ancient northern China.

nameMeanings.name: der
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Hungarian Zoltan, Istvan, Janos, Lajos.
Hungarian (Dér): from the old personal name Dér of Turkic origin, or a nickname for a gray-haired man, from the vocabulary word dér ‘hoarfrost’.
Chinese: variant Romanization of the surname 謝, see   Xie   1.

variants.name: Xie
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Hong, Li, Ming, Ying, Min, Qing, Yuan, Bin, Hui, Ping, Wei, Xiaoli, Ding, Chang, Lan, Quan, Cong, Dang, Hai, Tian, Yiping.

Mandarin form of the surname 謝, meaning ‘thank’ in Chinese: (i) from Xie (謝), the name of an ancient state (possibly located in Nanyang in Henan province), annexed around the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ).Later this place was probably granted to Shen Bo (the Earl of Shen), uncle of King Xuan of Zhou (reigned 827–782  BC ). After it was annexed during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ), the name of the state was adopted as a surname. (ii) adopted in place of the surname Zhi-Le (直勒) by Xie Xiaozheng (謝孝政), an official during the Northern Qi dynasty (550–577  AD ).

nameMeanings.name: Lede
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: topographic name for someone who lived near a loud, rushing stream, Old English hl̄de, or a habitational name from Lead in Yorkshire, which is named from Old English lǣd ‘water course’ or Old English hlēda ‘ledge’.

nameMeanings.name: Jan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: As a boy's name this represents a revival of Middle English Jan, a by‐form of John , or an adoption of the common European form with this spelling. As a girl's name it is a short form of names such as Janet and Janice .

variants.name: John
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

en.lifeSummary: When Jan I van der Lede was born in 1205, in Arkel, South Holland, Netherlands, his father, Floris Herbaren van der Lede, was 35 and his mother, Asekijn Huges Bottersdr van Schoonhoven, was 26. He married Jutta before 1230, in Europe. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 2 daughters. He died before 24 November 1272, in Netherlands.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Jan I van der Lede est né en 1205, à Arkel, Hollande-Méridionale, Pays-Bas, son père, Floris Herbaren van der Lede, avait 35 ans et sa mère, Asekijn Huges Bottersdr van Schoonhoven, avait 26 ans. Il a épousé Jutta avant 1230, à Europe. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé avant 24 novembre 1272, à Pays-Bas.

de.lifeSummary: Als Jan I van der Lede 1205 in Arkel, Südholland, Niederlande geboren wurde, war sein Vater Floris Herbaren van der Lede 35 und seine Mutter Asekijn Huges Bottersdr van Schoonhoven 26. Er hat vor 1230 in Europa Jutta geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 2 Töchter. Er starb vor 24. November 1272 in Niederlande.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Jan I van der Lede è nato il nel 1205 nel Arkel, Zuid-Holland, Paesi Bassi, il suo padre, Floris Herbaren van der Lede, aveva 35 anni e il suo madre, Asekijn Huges Bottersdr van Schoonhoven, aveva 26 anni. Lui sposato Jutta prima del 1230, nel Europa. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 2 figlie. Morì prima del 24 novembre 1272, nel Paesi Bassi.

ja.lifeSummary: Jan I van der Ledeが1205年にオランダズイド-hオランドアルケルで生まれた時，父Floris Herbaren van der Ledeは35歳，母Asekijn Huges Bottersdr van Schoonhovenは26歳であった。1230年以前にヨーロッパでJuttaと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼は1272年11月24日以前にオランダで死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Jan I van der Lede(이)가 1205년 네덜란드 주이드-홀랜드 아클에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Floris Herbaren van der Lede(은)는 35세였고, 그의 어머니 Asekijn Huges Bottersdr van Schoonhoven(은)는 26세였다. 그는 1230년 이전 우럽에서 Jutta(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1272년 11월 24일 이전 네덜란드에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Jan I van der Lede nasceu em 1205, em Arkel, Zuid-Holland, Países Baixos, seu pai, Floris Herbaren van der Lede, tinha 35 anos e sua mãe, Asekijn Huges Bottersdr van Schoonhoven, tinha 26 anos. Ele casou-se com Jutta antes 1230, em Europa. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 2 filhas. Ele faleceu antes 24 de novembro de 1272, em Países Baixos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Jan I van der Lede родился в 1205 г. в местности аркел, зуид-холланд, Нидерланды, его отцу по имени Floris Herbaren van der Lede было 35 лет, а матери, которую звали Asekijn Huges Bottersdr van Schoonhoven, 26. Он женился на женщине по имени Jutta перед 1230 г. в местности Европа. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 2 дочери. Он умер перед 24 ноябрь 1272 г. в местности Нидерланды.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Jan I van der Lede nació en 1205, en Arkel, Zuid-Holland, Países Bajos, su papá, Floris Herbaren van der Lede, tenía 35 años y su mamá, Asekijn Huges Bottersdr van Schoonhoven, tenía 26 años. Se casó con Jutta antes de 1230, en Europa. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 2 hijas. Murió antes de 24 de noviembre de 1272, en Países Bajos.
